title: Gentianella lingulata
taxonomy: Gentianella lingulata is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It belongs to the genus Gentianella which is a part of the Gentianaceae family. Gentianella lingulata is classified as a dicotyledonous plant with a perennial growth habit. This classification places it within the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. The species is further classified into the class Magnoliopsida and the order Gentianales.

intro: Gentianella lingulata, also known as Alpine gentian, is a small, perennial plant belonging to the gentian family (Gentianaceae). It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in digestion, alleviate respiratory issues, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Gentianella lingulata include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Gentianella lingulata is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or dried extract, often used to support digestive health and as an anti-inflammatory agent.
